articles.title: Stock Market Today (June 25, 2026): Mag7 falls after Apple, Microsoft announce price increases on iPhone, Xbox
articles.summary: S&P 500 closed at 7,357.49 (-0.01%), Nasdaq at 25,358.60 (-0.46%), Dow at 51,920.62 (+0.14%). Apple fell 6.13% after raising MacBook/iPad prices; Microsoft fell 3.23% after Xbox price hikes. Micron rose 15.78% on blowout Q3 earnings. PCE inflation 4.1% YoY (in line). Durable goods orders fell 4.5% MoM. Russell 2000 led with +0.71%.
articles.substantive_text: S&P 500: 7,357.49 (-0.01%), Nasdaq: 25,358.60 (-0.46%), Dow: 51,920.62 (+0.14%, up 71 pts), Russell 2000: ~3,000 (+0.38%), VIX: above 19 (+3%), 10Y Treasury: 4.394% (lowest since mid-May). AAPL -6.13% (MacBook Neo $599→$699, M3 Ultra Mac Studio $3,999→$5,299), MSFT -3.23% (Xbox 512GB +$100, 1TB +$150), MU +15.78% (Q3: $41.46B rev, $25.11 adj EPS vs $20.78 est), SNDK +18.25%, QCOM +10%, AYI +21.4%, BB +20.62%, TECH +19.47% (Merck KGaA $11.3B acquisition), CAT +5.81%, TCOM -13.48%. Headline PCE 4.1% YoY (in line), Core PCE 3.4% YoY (in line). Durable goods -4.5% MoM to $332.1B. WTI $69.47/bbl (-1.24%), Brent $72.62/bbl (-1.52%), Gold $4,002.20/oz (-0.16%). GOP Sens. Cassidy and Paul flipped votes on Iran War Powers Resolution.

articles.title: Dollar retreats as US inflation data eases rate hike expectations
articles.summary: DXY fell 0.19% to 101.41 after May PCE matched expectations at 4.1% YoY. July Fed hike odds dropped to ~30% from 34.2%. Jobless claims fell to 215K vs 225K expected. Consumer spending rose 0.7% MoM.
articles.substantive_text: DXY -0.19% to 101.41, largest daily drop in two weeks. EUR/USD +0.16% to $1.1375. GBP/USD +0.25% to $1.3196. USD/JPY +0.01% to 161.79 (break above 161.96 would be yen's weakest since 1986). Headline PCE 4.1% YoY (match), monthly PCE +0.4% (est 0.5%). Consumer spending May +0.7% (April +0.4%, forecast 0.6%). Q1 GDP revised to 2.1% from 1.6%. Jobless claims 215K (down 12K, forecast 225K). CME FedWatch: July hike ~30% (down from 34.2%), September 62.1% (down from 65.7%). Chicago Fed's Goolsbee: core inflation 'trending the wrong way.' Brian Jacobsen (Annex Wealth): 'worst of inflation may be mostly behind us.' BoJ's Tamura urges rate hikes every few months.

articles.title: Micron (MU) earnings report Q3 2026
articles.summary: Micron Q3 FY2026 (reported Jun 24 after-close, first full session reaction Jun 25): Revenue $41.46B (est $35.84B), adj EPS $25.11 (est $20.78), gross margin 84.9%. Q4 guidance ~$50B (est $43.58B). 16 long-term agreements for $22B in commitments. Stock rose 15% after hours, market cap past $1T.
articles.substantive_text: Revenue: $41.46B vs $35.84B estimate (345.8% YoY from $9.3B). Adj EPS: $25.11 vs $20.78 estimate. Net income: $28.24B ($24.46/share) vs $1.89B prior year. Gross margin: 84.9% vs 74.9% prior Q. Q4 guidance: ~$50B revenue (consensus $43.58B). Data Center: $11.5B (>7x), Cloud Memory: $13.77B (>300%), Mobile & Client: $11.52B (+250%), Automotive: $4.63B (>4x). 16 long-term customer agreements with $22B commitments. CEO Mehrotra: 'Supply shortages in memory and storage will take considerable time to improve.' CFO Murphy: 'We get visibility on demand, committed volume.' Stock rose 15% after hours, up ~700% over past year, market cap >$1T. AI-fueled memory crunch consuming nearly all production capacity.

articles.title: Xbox to raise console prices worldwide from August
articles.summary: Microsoft's Xbox raising prices Aug 1: 512GB +$100, 1TB +$150, 2TB discontinued. Third price hike in 13 months. Storage/memory chip costs surged 2.5x; expect another doubling by fall 2027.
articles.substantive_text: 512GB models +$100, 1TB models +$150, 2TB model discontinued. Effective Aug 1, 2026. Third increase in 13 months: May 2025 (+$100 Series X $500→$600), Oct 2025, now Jun 2026. Xbox statement: 'Console storage and memory prices have increased by more than 2.5 times and we expect another doubling by the fall of 2027.' AI datacenter buildout is consuming memory supply. Sony raised PS5 prices April 2026. Apple raised iPad/MacBook prices June 25 citing same memory cost pressure. Xbox planning major layoffs in July 2026 plus significant cuts to marketing budgets (per Bloomberg).

articles.title: Fed 'dot plot': Almost half of FOMC members project at least one interest rate hike this year
articles.summary: 9 of 18 FOMC members project at least one rate hike in 2026; 6 of those signal multiple hikes. Chair Warsh may discontinue the dot plot entirely. Rate cuts 'almost surely off the table' for 2026.
articles.substantive_text: 9 of 18 FOMC participants (excluding Chair Warsh) project ≥1 rate hike in 2026; 6 of 9 suggest multiple hikes possible. Remaining 9 see rates unchanged or lower. Median end-2027 forecast unchanged at 3.50-3.75%. March 2026 dot plot had median of one cut in 2026. June statement dropped easing-bias language. Warsh: 'I don't believe in forward guidance... There will be a review about communications broadly, press conferences, dots, meetings, and the like.' Gregory Daco (EY-Parthenon): 'This might be the last time we see the dot plot.' Rate cuts off the table for rest of 2026.

articles.source: Trading Economics / U.S. Dept of Labor
articles.title: United States Initial Jobless Claims
articles.summary: Initial jobless claims fell to 215K for week ending June 20 (vs 225K consensus), lowest in 4 weeks. Continuing claims rose to 1,821K (3-month high). Federal employee claims fell to 431.
articles.substantive_text: Initial claims: 215K (down 12K from 227K, consensus 225K), lowest in 4 weeks. Continuing claims: 1,821K (up 21K from 1,800K), highest in 3 months. Federal employee initial claims: 431 (down 61 from 497). 4-week moving average: 224.25K. Labor market characterized as 'low firing, low hiring.' Next release (week ending Jun 27) forecast: 210K. Claims remain historically robust. Continuing claims creeping up suggests workers taking longer to find new jobs.

articles.title: XAUUSD: Gold's $4,000 Break Is a Rate Story, Not a War Story
articles.summary: Gold broke below $4,000/oz on June 24 for first time since Nov 2025. 29% drawdown from January record ~$5,600. Hawkish Fed dot plot, elevated real yields, strong dollar, unwinding geopolitical risk premium.
articles.substantive_text: XAUUSD broke below $4,000/oz June 24, first time since Nov 2025. Vantage CFD last near $3,982.66. 29% drawdown from Jan record ~$5,600. Fed held at 3.50-3.75% June 17 under Chair Warsh, median dot rose to 3.8% (from 3.4% March). All easing-bias language removed. DXY pushed above 101. Goldman Sachs cut year-end 2026 gold forecast from $5,400 to $4,900. Deutsche Bank: Q3 $4,300, Q4 base $4,800, downside $3,800 if Fed hikes 3-4 times. US-Iran 60-day peace roadmap signed. Brent crude fell to lowest since late Feb. Key: 'primarily a real-rates and dollar event. Much of geopolitical risk premium has unwound. The Fed has not.' Technical: RSI(14) 45.74, 200-MA 3,990.91, both MAs declining above price. Support: 3,940-3,960, Resistance: 3,990-4,000.

articles.title: Crude Oil WTI Futures Historical Prices
articles.summary: WTI crude settled at $71.47 (+1.61% on day). 52-week range $54.98-$117.63. Down 26% over past month from $97 peak on Jun 3. Day's range $68.91-$72.50.
articles.substantive_text: WTI Jun 25 close: $71.47 (+1.61%). Open $69.85, high $72.50, low $68.91. Volume 207,296. 52-week range: $54.98-$117.63. Over past 30 days: fell from $97.00 (Jun 3 high) to $68.91 (Jun 25 low), a 26% decline. Largest single-day gain: Jun 1 (+5.49%). Largest single-day loss: Jun 15 (-6.41%). Strait of Hormuz shipping resumed under US-Iran MoU. Midday spike +2% after reports of Iranian IRGC attack on Singaporean vessel in strait.

articles.title: Daily Treasury Yield Curve Rates - June 25, 2026
articles.summary: 10Y Treasury yield at 4.394% (lowest since mid-May per TheStreet). 30Y at 4.85% (+0.01pp). Yield curve methodology updated Dec 2021 to monotone convex spline.
articles.substantive_text: 10Y Treasury: 4.394% (lowest since mid-May, per TheStreet context). 30Y: 4.85% (+0.01pp per Trading Economics). Curve methodology: monotone convex spline (MC) since Dec 6, 2021. Daily Treasury Bill Rates and Yield Curve data available from Treasury.gov. Data as of Thursday Jun 25, 2026.

articles.title: Merck KGaA to Acquire Bio-Techne for $11.3B
articles.summary: Merck KGaA (Darmstadt, Germany) agrees to acquire Bio-Techne (TECH) for $11.3B enterprise value, $73/share cash, 24% premium. Brings proteomics, spatial biology, cell therapy capabilities.
articles.substantive_text: Merck KGaA to acquire Bio-Techne Corp. (Minneapolis) for ~$11.3B enterprise value. $73/share cash, 24% premium. Bio-Techne: >3,000 employees, 34 global locations, 15 manufacturing facilities, FY2025 sales >$1.2B. Portfolio: cytokines, growth factors, antibodies, immunoassay kits, ProteinSimple instruments, RNAscope spatial biology, cell therapy materials. Merck KGaA CEO Kai Beckmann: 'Important milestone towards delivering on our mid- to long-term strategic agenda.' Bio-Techne CEO Kim Kelderman: 'Testament to the remarkable company our team has built.'

articles.title: Market Internals: Breadth, Sector Moves, Apple/Microsoft Price Hikes (full context)
articles.summary: Detailed market internals and broader sector movements. Apple's Tim Cook told WSJ the company can no longer shield customers from component costs. Industrials, healthcare, financials led the Dow higher.
articles.substantive_text: AAPL: Tim Cook told WSJ 'increasingly unable to shield customers from rising component costs.' iPad $349→$449, MacBook Neo $599→$699, M3 Ultra Mac Studio $3,999→$5,299. MSFT: Third Xbox price hike in 13 months. GOOGL will join Dow on Jun 29 replacing VZ. CAT +5.81%, UNH +2.65%, GS +1.15% led Dow. Industrials, health care, financials provided broad support. Analyst Daniela Hathorn (Capital.com): 'Micron's earnings have provided fresh reassurance that the AI investment cycle remains firmly intact.' Chevron CFO expects pump prices to fall as Middle East stabilizes. Trump ordered investigation into Big Oil for gouging. WEN -3.4% giving back prior day's ~25% meme rally (r/WSB). HTZ -6.1%, DLTR -3.6%, DVA -3.3%, ALB -4.4%, PSNY -11.65% (withdrawing from US market).

articles.title: CBOE Volatility Index (^VIX) Historical Data
articles.summary: VIX closed at 18.89 on June 25 (+1.40% from prior close 18.63). Day's range: 17.43-19.95. Elevated but below panic levels. March 2026 peak was 31.05 close; 52-week low 13.47 (Dec 2025).
articles.substantive_text: VIX Jun 25: Open 18.11, High 19.95, Low 17.43, Close 18.89 (+1.40% from 18.63 prior close). 52-week low: 13.47 (Dec 24, 2025). 52-week high close: 31.05 (Mar 27, 2026). Intraday high: 35.30 (Mar 9, 2026). March-April 2026 was major volatility surge with multiple closes above 25. May-June 2026: receded to 15-22 range. Current 18.89 is elevated relative to calm periods but well below the March stress peaks.

articles.title: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread
articles.summary: High yield OAS at 2.71% as of June 23 (previous day 2.65%, year-ago 3.12%). Next release June 25. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity volatility.
articles.substantive_text: HY OAS: 2.71% (Jun 23), up from 2.65% prior day. Year ago: 3.12%. Source: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread via FRED. Next release: Jun 25. HYG (iShares HY Corp Bond ETF): spread 250 bps as of Jun 24, yield 5.92% as of Jun 23, avg yield to maturity 6.92%. IG OAS (Macrotrends): 0.74% as of Jun 23. Credit markets showing contained stress relative to March volatility spike.

articles.title: Geopolitics: Iran War Powers Resolution, Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: GOP Sens. Cassidy and Paul flipped votes on Iran War Powers Resolution after contentious White House meeting. US-Iran MoU signed June 17 sets 60-day negotiation period. Oil spiked midday on Iranian IRGC attack on Singaporean vessel.
articles.substantive_text: GOP Sens. Cassidy and Paul flipped their votes after White House meeting with President Trump on Iran War Powers Resolution. US-Iran MoU signed June 17 for 60-day negotiation period. Strait of Hormuz shipping has resumed, but midday oil spike (+2%) followed reports of Iranian IRGC attack on a Singaporean vessel in the strait — underscoring fragile ceasefire. Oil prices slid back to near pre-conflict levels on the shipping resumption but the incident injected fresh uncertainty.

articles.title: United States 30 Year Bond Yield
articles.summary: US 30Y bond yield at 4.85% (+0.01pp from prior session). Dollar index DXY at 101.41 (-0.19%). EUR/USD 1.1375, GBP/USD 1.3196, USD/JPY 161.79.
articles.substantive_text: 30Y: 4.85% (+0.01pp). DXY: 101.41 (-0.19%), largest daily drop in two weeks, snapping 3-session winning streak. Dollar had hit 13-month peak previous day. EUR/USD: 1.1375 (+0.16%). GBP/USD: 1.3196 (+0.25%), recovering after PM Starmer resignation-driven declines. USD/JPY: 161.79 (+0.01%). Break above 161.96 would be weakest yen since 1986. Gold briefly below $4,000/oz for first time in 7+ months. Bitcoin briefly below $60,000 (first time since early June).
